title: Perplexity & Evaluation
content:
# Interview Prep: Perplexity

## Q1: Prove how perplexity corresponds to the average branching factor of a language model.

### Standard Answer
Consider a language model predicting the next token from a vocabulary of size $V$. If the model is completely uniform (i.e. $P(w_i \mid \text{context}) = \frac{1}{K}$ for exactly $K$ words, and $0$ for others), the perplexity is:

$$\text{PPL}(W) = \left( \prod_{i=1}^N \frac{1}{K} \right)^{-\frac{1}{N}} = \left( K^{-N} \right)^{-\frac{1}{N}} = K$$

Thus, a perplexity of $K$ indicates that the model is as confused as if it had to choose uniformly from $K$ candidates at each step. $K$ represents the **average branching factor** of the language.
